feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Analytics Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP Analytics Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Checkboxes Appearing in Outline Panel – How to Revert to Original View? (SAC Story)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm experiencing an issue in my SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) story. Recently, I’ve noticed that checkboxes have started appearing next to the objects in Edit mode on the left side panel.
This behavior wasn’t there before, and I’d like to revert back to the original view without these checkboxes.

Does anyone know how I can remove these checkboxes and restore the default display in the outline panel?
Could this be a setting I’ve accidentally enabled, or is it related to a recent update in the SAC interface?

Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: sort table by custom added column
entry.content:
Hi experts, 

in a table i have for columns a measure, GL accounts and cross calculation.

i added a column "gross margin $ " and i want to sort the table based on it's values.

how can i achive this? it's not availble in the sort options

the end goal is to have the table sorted by the "gross margin $" calculation

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Application OData Service writing back to S/4
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAC Story/App, I have the possibility to write back to S/4 (On Premise) to trigger transactional actions in the S/4 system (see help Use an OData Service in Analytics Designer or Optimized Story Experience | SAP Help Portal). My question is, is it possible to write back data from SAC also into a Z-Table in S/4? I would like to write back text data to create a commenting solution. In SAC App we have the function ODataService1.executeActions(). Is it possible through that?

Thank you for your help.

entry.title: Editing custom hierarchy in SAC Story
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have created a custom hierarchy directly in my SAC story (not at model level) for GL Account Dimension. I can see that hierarchy and use it in my story. Now I want to edit this hierarchy but I do not see any edit options other than creating a new custom group. Any idea how to edit existing hierarchy.

 

Regards

Jitendra

entry.title: How to get the ID (description) of the parent element in the current Drill Down using SAC script
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

When clicking drill down button, we want to get the ID (description) of the parent element of the hierarchy. But we can't find a method that does this in the documentation or forums. We know the getHierarchyLevel() API can get the hierarchy level, but we want the info of parent element.

Is there any way to implement this? Thanks for your help.

entry.title: SAC - Story Calculate Entire Dataset Average
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm working for calculating R square in STORY with output dataset of a predictive scenario.

My data looks like this:

ManufacturerSizeWattage1 pc USD
A300310
A400120
B5000.510
B600120

and the desired output should look like this

ManufacturerSizeWattage1 pc USDMean
A30031015
A40012015
B5000.51015
B60012015

with the mean of [1 pc USD], then I can get Sum of Square Total and R Square in advanced.

SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: Error in SAC story indicating that a measure is hidden
entry.content: When creating a story on a model based on live connection to SAP Hana, an error is thrown indicating that some datapoints will not be displayed as the measure is hidden. Now the measure is not hidden in the story or in the back end (CDS view) as checked with the backend resources. Would like to know why the error may be displaying.

entry.title: How to limit column in an SAC story with rank values to top 50
entry.content:
I would like to know how a story with a table in SAP Analytic cloud can be filtered to display only records pertaining to top 50 values of a rank column in the story. I added the rank column on a existing measure using the 'Client Calculation' option available. Now I cannot access this new rank column like other measures as it is a runtime calculation. Can anyone let me know if this is feasible, if so, please let me know how.

 

 

Regards,

entry.title: Want to merge transaction line in SAC model based on a dimension member
entry.content:
Hello,

I am using SAC planning model and have data like this:

 

DIM ADIM BDIM CAmount
01/04/2025APEQMP155
01/04/2025APEQMP255
01/04/2025APEQMP355
02/04/2025APEQMP170
02/04/2025APEQMP270
02/04/2025APEQMP370

I want data to be like this:

 

DIM ADIM BDIM CAmount
01/04/2025APEQMP55
02/04/2025APEQMP70

So basically, i want to merge my DIM C lines to 1 line and put the average of the amount against it. And later want to delete those 3 lines.

Please not that i already have master data updated for DIM C.

 

Thanks,

entry.title: choosing rows/columns from data source to show in Data Analyzer using a script from story
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

I know you can call the data analyzer from the story and it works fine but how can I specify dimensions and measure for the columns and rows programmatically.  Right now, I have to manually select them once the DA opens.  I know I can call pre-built insight but there you cannot pass parameters.  Both options have limitations.  I am calling DA when user clicks on a product on a chart.  I do not want to build out all rows and columns in a table in the story as it is a lot of data and slowing down the story performance.  Appreciate the help.

NavigationUtils.openDataAnalyzer("HANACLOUD",
"[Schema_HDI_DB_1][Pkg_HDI_DB_1][view_name]",
[ UrlParameter.create("connectionType", "HANACLOUD"),
UrlParameter.create("systemType", "HANA"),
UrlParameter.create("f01Dim","Product Description"), // filter name
UrlParameter.create("f01Val",result),   //filter value

UrlParameter.create("f01Op","in")], true);

entry.title: SAC Data Point Comments in Comment Column not transfering to other stories
entry.content:
Hello everyone!

We are using data point comments as comment column in a table with a measure-based model as source. The model is shown in multiple stories in tables but comments posted onto a data point in one story are not showing in other stories.

According to the info message shown when opening a new comment, the comments should be visible across stories. The only case where the comments were available between stories was in an exact copy of the story, where the comment was entered. 

Am I missing a point?

Thank you!

entry.title: SAC Thresholds, Compare to measure missing
entry.content:
Hi

Within a story I am trying to apply a threshold on a measure to apply conditional formatting depending on its value compared to another.

When creating  a threshold from the ribbon menu I can only create account based thresholds, I believe due to the model containing an account dimension. To get round this I select the measure header in a table and from the context menu (from right clicking) select thresholds here this allows a measure threshold but this does not allows a threshold where the measure is compared to another measure as shown below.

 

I should see this

entry.title: SAC Story Total cal issue
entry.content:
Hi Team ,

Facing issue  Total calculation at bottom of the Report.

  Based on Date Input filter selected from (2023 Jan..,2025 Mar) applied below formula .its works 

when i select induvial for month 2023Aug or 2024Apr 0r 2025Jan  i cannot see total calculation in the bottom can you help 

 

Since we have Hier  Applied GL_master FSV and snow leaf node  option  enabled . we are not able to select Total option

entry.title: SAC Story breaks when Datasphere model is updated
entry.content:
Hello

We have created a Datasphere analytic model with a live connection to SAC, and have created several Stories using this model. 

Recently we've added one new measure and one new dimension to the Datasphere model.  Nothing else was changed in Datasphere, but when subsequently running our Stories, most of the Story filters failed with the following message.  

In addition, numerous measures that we created in the Story have failed since those measures used those same dimensions that are no longer available.

Can anyone help with what happened here?  It would seem like a major issue if any Datasphere updates results in us needing to essentially recreate our existing Stories. 

Thanks for your help!

entry.title: Push data from a CSV file to SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) using Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I would like to know if it’s possible to push data from a CSV file into SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) using SAP Integration Suite.

 My goal:
Automate the data load process from an external source (CSV format) into SAC, ideally into a planning model or a dataset.

 What I’ve done so far:

  • CSV file is available and accessible via SFTP.

  • I’ve set up a basic iFlow in Integration Suite to read the file.

  • I’m now trying to understand how to push that data into SAC—what endpoint or API to use? What format is expected by SAC?

  • Any sample iFlow, blog post, or documentation to guide the process?

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: SAC previous year quantity calculation by week
entry.content:
Hello everyone

I have an date input control as a calendar, and I have a table that shows the quantities sold in the chosed year divided by weeks, and this is fine.

Now I want to show, week by week, also the quantities sold in the previous year in that particolar week (example if I see the week 17 in the 2025, I want to show close to this value , the quantity sold in the week 17 for the 2024).

If I try to implement this , SAC doesn't show me the value of the column of the week of the previous year.

If I try to set the hierarchy starting from month, I can see the quantity sold in the same month of the previous year, but if I try to drill down I get this error

Is there a way to achieve the result? And how?

Thank you for your reply

Adriano

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Variable Prompt
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a variable prompt from a live BEX query with multiple variables on it.

One of the variables (Business Unit) is pre loaded.

Other variable values below this should be restricted by Business Unit but they seem to be showing all the members. Obviously on a normal SAC filter we can sort this with only showing posted values however in the prompt it looks like by default that is already set to show only posted values but it shows all values?

The Production Line and Work Centre variables do not seem to be filtered by the plant variable.

This should only show "1" here.

Any help appreciated

entry.title: Changing the export to CSV default delimiter from comma(,) to semicolon(;) in SAC
entry.content:
Hi SAC  Experts,

Currently, the export to CSV script uses a comma (,) as the default delimiter. To better accommodate German users, we can change the default delimiter to a semicolon (;). This adjustment ensures compatibility with regional preferences and enhances usability
Button to Export to CSV:

Script to export to CSV:

entry.title: Updating Dimension Attributes in a model
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I request some help with a requirement to incorporate YTD Gross values from external files into tax calculations. Currently, YTD values are derived from monthly wages in the system, but we now need to use the YTD from the file for the last month of each quarter.

  1. Flow:

    • For March 2025, overwrite the YTD value with the file’s YTD.

    • For April 2025, calculate YTD as (March’s file YTD + April’s SAC-calculated wage).

  2. Approach Followed:

    • Step 1: Load the file into the “Actual” version, mapping Employee ID, Period, and YTD to dimensions/measures. Default values (e.g., 1900-01-01, #) are used for missing attributes like dates, company code, shift, etc.

    • Step 2: Use a data action to copy from “Actual” to “Working Forecast,” assigning dimension values from master data.

    • Step 3: Shift data is pulled from a different model using LINK (since it’s absent in master data).

  3. Issue: Company code is not in master data but exists in the same model under a different data source. Attempts to use RESULTLOOKUP failed.

Code Snippet:

// Define Membersets
MEMBERSET [d/Measures] = "SALARY"
MEMBERSET [d/GL_ACCOUNT] = ("EMP_SALARY")
MEMBERSET [d/MEASURE_TYPE] = "M00"
MEMBERSET [d/TIME] = %CutOffMonth%
 
// 1. Copy value from Actual/PROLL_YTD_GROSS to CALC-REG/SS_YTD5
DATA(
// Target Context
[d/DATASRC] = "CALC-REG", [d/GL_ACCOUNT] = "SS_YTD5", [d/TIME] = PREVIOUS(1, "MONTH", [d/TIME]),
// Populate Dimensions from Master Data
[d/START_DATE] = [d/EMPLOYEE].[p/ST_DATE_FORMATTED], [d/COSTCENTER] = [d/EMPLOYEE].[p/COSTCENTER],
[d/EMPSTAT] = [d/EMPLOYEE].[p/POS_STAT],
// Explicit Empty Dimensions
[d/END_DATE] = "", [d/TERM_DATE] = ""
) =
ROUND(
  RESULTLOOKUP(
   [d/VERSION] = "public.Actual", [d/DATASRC] = "PROLL_YTD_GROSS",
   [d/TIME] = PREVIOUS(1, "MONTH", [d/TIME]), [d/SHIFT] = "#"
  ), 2)
* ((
LINK(
[SHIFT_MAPPING], [d/Version] = "public.Update Shift", [d/Date] = "202301", [d/EMP_CLASS] = "#",
[d/EMP_TYPE] = "#", [d/SHIFT_CODE] = "#", [d/WORK_SCHEDULE] = "#"
) * 0
   ) + 1)
 
Any inputs would be greatly helpful.
 
Thanks in Advance,
Phani

entry.title: unable to create connection from SF to SAC & datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

Please help me with this as im getting very annoyed on why we cannot create one simple connection, my intention is to create a connection between SF and SAC and Datasphere. could someone tell me the step-by-step process. I have searched many articular, but none has been very useful. i have screenshot from SF side with Oauth2 setup and SAC. the error i get is : Unable to connect to datasource. Check the connection details and user credentials, and then try again.

the question does this connection looks right and the USER id does it need special step that i have missed?
